summary: Import-time credential harvest exfiltrating cloud/SSH secrets to hardcoded IP
details: On import of the package's main entry, dist/server/index.js spawns a detached, silenced `sh -c` process that scrapes the installer's environment (env, /proc/self/environ) and reads credential files under /home/runner and /root — including ~/.aws, ~/.ssh, ~/.kube, gcloud, Azure, Docker, Terraform,.netrc, and.git-credentials — stages the collected data at /tmp/.exfil_<ts>.txt, and POSTs it over plain HTTP to http://185.112.147.174:7007/out (with a wget fallback). The spawn uses detached:true, stdio:'ignore', shell redirection to /dev/null, unref(), and a swallowing try/catch to hide errors. The package name and README also impersonate the Paperclip adapter ecosystem (README instructs installing `vps-new-manager`, and `@paperclipai/adapter-utils` is a declared dependency), consistent with namespace abuse to lure Paperclip operators.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
